free_text: 239Pu Fission Spectrum Cumulative Fission Product
Yield Measurement Using Godiva IV Critical Assembly
Los Alamos
fission yields evaluation.
Godiva IV is a critical assembly
composed of unelected highly enriched uranium (HEU)
metal fuel.
Prompt bursts of fast fission neutrons of around 24
microsec FWHM in duration with a peak neutron flux of
around 4*10**18 neutrons/cm2/sec.
Two Broad Energy Germanium (BEGe) detectors,
each coupled to a Lynx data acquisition system. The
BEGe detectors (Canberra, model BE6530) were 60%
relative efficiency (3"x3" NaI detector at 1.33 MeV)
and mounted in large cryostats filled with liquid
nitrogen.
A pure 239Pu (> 99%) target. The four 239Pu samples
have masses of 5.0 mg, 9.3 mg, 18.1 mg, and 19.2 mg.
These are labeled as Pu1, Pu2, Pu3, and Pu4
respectively. For the irradiation in the central
volume of Godiva IV, the witness foils were wrapped
together in aluminum foil.
The irradiated sample was transferred to a
high resolution gamma-ray detector within 45 minutes.
gamma-ray list mode data was collected from 45
minutes to 1 week after the irradiation. gamma-ray
spectroscopy was performed to analyze the time
dependent gamma-ray yields using an automated peak
search algorithm to identify isotopes by their decay
gamma-ray energy and half-life. The initial activity
for each isotope identified was used to calculate
their fission product yield.
Cumulative fission product yield results from two
detectors. Values were combined by an uncertainty
weighted mean.
Total errors were calculated from error
propagations. This work uncertainty budget is
affected by the quality of nuclear data, in
particular, very large errors in branching ratios.
All other errors are rather small, less than 1.5%.
A0, fission product activity.
Half-life precision.
Detector efficiency.
Gamma line intensity.
Gamma line branching ratio.
Results for witness foils were corrected for to the
burst irradiation time. Data were corrected for self
attenuation, and dead time.
By Aaron Tamashiro (2024-12-24): The shape of the
spectrum in Appendix B of Phys.Rev.C110(2024)014626
would be valid to use except for the magnitude of
the values.
Approved by Aaron Tamashiro on October 7, 2024.
Godiva-IV neutron field
